DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCES: CARY SAMOWITZ AND GREG JUNG FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY: BDO SEIDMAN, LLP AND FRANCOIS HECHINGER NANCY L. FINEMAN AND ALI ABTAHI FOR FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY: ERIC MATHEWSON HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO EXCLUDE AND SEAL THE "KEREKES MEMORANDUM" IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DOCUMENT IS AN ATTORNEY CLIENT COMMUNICATION AND IS A PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE PRIVILEGE HAS NOT BEEN KNOWINGLY WAIVED. MODIFICATION: WHILE THE COURT CANNON UNRING THE BELL AS THE DOCUMENT HAS COME INTO THE PUBLIC RECORD OF OTHER COURTS, AND WHILE THE DOCUMENT WAS PRODUCED IN A MANNER THAT HAS BEEN THE SUBJECT OF DISPUTE IN DIFFERENT JURISDICTIONS, THIS COURT MUST RULE UPON THE ADMISSIBILITY OF THE DOCUMENT IN THIS CASE. THIS COURT DETERMINES THAT THE PRODUCTION, WICH RESULTED IN THE DOCUMENT BEING OBTAINED BY PLAINTIFF, WAS NOT A KNOWING AND VOLUNTARY PRODUCTION OR WAIVER. AS THE ISSUE HAS BEEN PRESENTED TO THIS COURT FOR DETERMINATION, THIS COURT HAS THE OBLICATION TO RULE UPON THE QUESTION OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO USE THE DOCUMENT IN THIS PROCEEDING. THIS COURT DOES DETERMINE THAT THE ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE BARS THE USE OF THE DOCUMENT IN THIS LITIGATION. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT
